title: Breastfeeding Support Programs - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Health Care > Breastfeeding Support Programs 49 Matching Service Providers 211INFO 211INFO MATERNAL AND CHILD HEALTH PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF 211info's Maternal and Child Health Program provides specialized information, referral, and follow-up services to individuals and family members of all genders covering needs and questions related to pregnancy, maternal and child health, sexual and reproductive health, and women's health across the life span, including resources for women with breast cancer. Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-6pm Email 211 Visit Website More Details ADVENTIST HEALTH ADVENTIST HEALTH PORTLAND BIRTH, LABOR, DELIVERY CLASSES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Classes provide information about all options about birth, labor, delivery, breastfeeding, and caring for a newborn. 10123 SE Market Street Portland, OR 97216 Eligibility: Pregnant parents Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (503) 256-4000 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ASANTE ASANTE ASHLAND COMMUNITY HOSPITAL LACTATION SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides lactation support services for all stages of breastfeeding. Patients who receive prenatal care at NHC will work with providers to make delivery arrangements at Hillsboro Medical Center. Prenatal care services include routine scheduled exams, lactation education and support, along with birth planning, postpartum, and well baby care. 10690 NE Cornell Road Suite 220 Hillsboro, OR 97124 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 7am-6pm (503) 848-5861 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OFFICE ON WOMEN'S HEALTH NATIONAL WOMEN'S HEALTH INFORMATION CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides current information on women's health issues both over the phone and on a website. Offers publications on various health topics such as pregnancy, breastfeeding, body image, HIV/AIDS, menopause, quitting smoking and violence against women. Information is available for special populations: minority women, women with disabilities, girls, and men. Referrals can be made to appropriate national health organizations. 200 Independence Avenue SW Room 712 E Washington, DC 20201 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 6am-3pm Email (800) 994-9662 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OHSU CENTER FOR WOMEN'S HEALTH CENTER FOR WOMEN’S HEALTH CENTER FOR WOMEN'S HEALTH SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides obstetric and gynecological services. Services include breast exams and mammograms, prenatal, childbirth, and postpartum care, lactation support, annual gynecological exams, STI/STD testing and treatment, contraceptives, tubal ligation services, and abortion services. For male sterilization (vasectomy), contact the Urology Clinic at 503-346-1500. 808 SW Campus Drive 7th Floor Portland, OR 97239 Eligibility: Women of any age Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (503) 418-4500 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OHSU CENTER FOR WOMEN'S HEALTH CENTER FOR WOMEN’S HEALTH CHILDBIRTH AND PARENTING PREPARATION CLASSES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a variety of prenatal, childbirth, newborn care, breastfeeding education, and infant safety classes.
